question: Q: What is "synapse"? (https://synapse.net)
answer:
A:
"synapse" is a specialized B2B platform for Japanese pharmaceutical information and procurement, operated by Kosei Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. It serves as a digital bridge between the Japanese drug market and global healthcare professionals.
Service Overview & Core Functions

    Pharmaceutical Database: A comprehensive, English-language database of Japanese medicines, searchable by Active Ingredient (USAN/INN), Therapeutic Class, or Manufacturer.

    Key Features:

        Search & Research: Accurate, professional-grade research for Japanese pharmaceutical products.

        Request for Quote (RFQ): Fast quotation generation via the "My List" feature.

        One-Stop Procurement: Seamless transition from information browsing to secure online ordering for registered members.

Target Audience

Specifically designed for overseas medical professionals, including Doctors, Pharmacists, Hospitals, and Licensed Pharmaceutical Wholesalers.

question: Q: What kind of company is Kosei Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd.?
answer:
A:
Kosei Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. is a premier Japanese pharmaceutical wholesaler and specialized trading house established in 1952. With over 70 years of history, the company is a global leader in supplying high-quality Japanese pharmaceuticals to the international market.
Corporate Profile & Strategic Strengths

    Core Business Integration: Combines a robust domestic foundation (trading with approx. 12,000 medical institutions in Japan) with an extensive global supply chain reaching over 50 countries.

    Proven Track Record: Maintains a vast network of over 70,000 corporate and medical partners worldwide.

    Specialized Expertise:

        Exporting Japanese-origin pharmaceuticals.

        Sourcing Orphan Drugs and Oncology medications.

        Operation of the global procurement platform "synapse".

    Corporate Governance: A wholly-owned subsidiary of Clinifer Co., Ltd. (Tokyo Stock Exchange: 432A). This ensures the highest standards of financial transparency and regulatory compliance.

question: Q: What specific features and functions are available on "synapse"?
answer:
A:
"synapse" provides an advanced, end-to-end digital ecosystem that streamlines the entire process from pharmaceutical research to procurement.
Core Features & Workflow

    Precision Search Engine: Enables pinpoint product identification through multi-dimensional filtering, including Active Ingredient (USAN/INN), Therapeutic Category, Manufacturer, and specialized tags.

    Streamlined Quotation Process (RFQ):

        "My List" Integration: Easily add required products to a centralized interest list.

        One-Click Request: Submit a free quotation request directly from your list.

        Fast Turnaround: Receive an accurate official quotation within as little as 2 business days.

    Digital Order Management: Seamlessly transition from quotation approval to final order placement and real-time progress tracking—all within the platform.

    Personalized Dashboard: A dedicated member portal to manage transaction history and optimize the monitoring of frequently sourced products.

question: Q: Is "synapse" available for use by anyone?
answer:
A:
No. To ensure the safety and integrity of pharmaceutical distribution, "synapse" is a restricted B2B platform exclusively for Licensed Wholesalers and Healthcare Professionals.
Eligibility & Verification Process

    Target Users: Doctors, Pharmacists, Hospital Administrators, Licensed Pharmaceutical Wholesalers, and Patients with a valid medical certificate.

    Membership Fee: Free of charge.

    Mandatory Verification Steps:

        Identity & Credential Proof: To access quoting and ordering features, users must submit and receive approval for a valid pharmaceutical handling license or professional certification.

    Compliance & Safety: This rigorous verification process prevents illicit trade in international medicine distribution and ensures a secure, transparent supply chain.

question: Q: How do I create an account on "synapse"?
answer:
A:
You can create your "synapse" account via the "Sign up" menu on the official website (https://synapse.net). The process is structured to ensure a secure professional environment.
Step-by-Step Registration Process

    Enter Information: Click the "Sign up" button in the top right corner of the screen and fill in the required fields, including name, contact details, and organization name.

    Account Activation: Follow the instructions in the verification email sent to your registered address to complete your profile setup.

    License Verification (Full Feature Unlock): To enable Quoting and Ordering functions, you must upload your Pharmaceutical Handling License or professional certification via your dashboard.

    Review and Approval: Once our administration team verifies your credentials, you will receive full access to all platform features.

question: Q: How can I reset my password if I forget it?
answer:
A:
If you have lost or forgotten your "synapse" login password, you can easily reset it by following these steps.
Password Reset Process

    Initiate Request: Click the "Forgot your password?" link on the Login page.

    Receive Email: Enter your registered email address and submit. A password reset link will be automatically sent to you.

    Set New Password: Click the link in the email and follow the instructions to create a new, secure password.

Troubleshooting (If you don't receive the email)

    Check Spam/Junk Folder: Ensure the automated email was not accidentally filtered by your email provider.

    Whitelist Domain: Add "synapse.net" to your safe senders list or ensure your server is set to receive our emails.

    Contact Support: If the issue persists after checking the above, please reach out via our [Inquiry Form] or contact customer support directly. Our dedicated team will assist you promptly.

question: Q: Are official documents required to create an account and use the features?
answer:
A:
Submission of documents is not required at the initial sign-up stage. However, to ensure the secure and legal distribution of pharmaceuticals, valid licensing is mandatory to unlock transaction features.
Phase-Based Requirements

    Account Creation Phase: Registration can be completed using only basic information, such as your name, company name, and email address.

    Feature Unlock (Critical): To access the following professional features, you must upload a "Pharmaceutical License" (or equivalent official certification) and receive administrative approval:

        Request for Quote (RFQ)

        Online Ordering

    Purpose of Submission: These requirements are strictly enforced to maintain international regulatory compliance and to guarantee the integrity and safety of the pharmaceutical supply chain.

question: Q: How can I change or update my account information?
answer:
A:
You can view and edit your registered information at any time via the "My Account" page after logging in.
Information Management & Access Levels

    Editable Information: Most basic profile details, such as contact information, job title, and notification settings, can be updated instantly by the user.

    Update Procedure: 1.  Log in to "synapse".
    2.  Select "My Account" from the navigation menu.
    3.  Update the necessary fields and click "Save".

question: Q: How can I efficiently search for specific pharmaceuticals on "synapse"?
answer:
A:
"synapse" features a multidimensional search system with advanced filtering capabilities. You can quickly identify the most suitable products using the following four primary search methods.
Search Indices & Functions

    Product Name & Keyword Search: Enter the brand name or generic name directly into the search bar.

    Active Ingredient Search: List all related products by searching for a specific Active Ingredient (USAN/INN).

    Therapeutic Category Search: Narrow down results based on pharmacological classification or specific therapeutic purposes.

    Tag Search: Explore products using associated tags such as disease names, specific drug characteristics, or pharmaceutical manufacturers.

If a Product is Not Found

Even if a product is not currently listed in our online database, it may still be available for sourcing. Please use the "Contact Us" or "Message" feature to provide the specific product name and specifications; our team will conduct a manual investigation for you.

question: Q: How do I request a quotation for Japanese pharmaceuticals?
answer:
A:
On "synapse", you can easily request a free quotation (Request for Quote / RFQ) by following these four steps.
Quotation Request Workflow

    Select & Add Products: Identify your required pharmaceuticals using the search engine, select the specific Packaging, and add them to your "My List."

    Centralized Management: You can consolidate multiple different medicines into "My List" to request a single, bulk quotation for efficiency.

    Specify Quantities: Enter the required quantity for each item within "My List," then proceed to the "Request List" and click the "Request" button.

    Set Conditions & Submit: Select your preferred Currency and Shipping Address. After a final review, submit your request. Our administration team will then process and issue your official quotation.

question: Q: What is the procedure for placing an actual order after confirming the quotation?
answer:
A:
Once you have received and approved your quotation, you can finalize your Online Order directly through the platform by following these steps.
Order & Checkout Workflow

    Review Quotation Results: Access your issued Quotation Results via the "My Account" dashboard to review pricing and terms.

    Verify Shopping Cart: Products from your approved quotation are automatically synchronized and pre-loaded into your Shopping Cart.

    Final Verification: In the cart, perform a final check of the product quantities and ensure that your registered Shipping Address is accurate.

    Confirm & Place Order: Conduct a final review of the total amount and terms on the confirmation screen, then click "Place Order" to complete the transaction.

question: Q: Is it possible to communicate directly with a representative regarding my orders or quotations?
answer:
A:
Yes. You can communicate seamlessly with a dedicated representative through our integrated messaging system on the platform.
Key Benefits of Our Communication System

    Context-Linked Messaging: Every quotation request and order comes with its own dedicated message thread. This ensures all conversations are perfectly synchronized with the specific transaction.

    Common Inquiries:

        Documentation: Confirmation and adjustment of Proforma Invoices (PI) or invoices.

question: Q: Can I easily place a "Re-order" for a product I have purchased before?
answer:
A:
Yes. You can quickly Re-order products from your transaction history within your "My Account" dashboard.
Re-order Specifications & Workflow

    Eligibility: Products from order history confirmed within the last 3 months are eligible for the simplified Re-order function.

    Steps to Re-order:

        Navigate to the "Order History" page.

        Select the relevant past order.

        Click "Re-order" or "Add to Cart" to initiate the checkout process.

Important Considerations

    Inventory Fluctuations: Availability may differ from your previous order. The system will perform a real-time check upon re-ordering.

    Supply Verification: For products marked as "Shortage" or those with unstable supply, a new confirmation of lead time and availability may be required, even for repeat orders.

    Orders Older Than 3 Months: If the product was purchased more than 3 months ago, please initiate a new Request for Quote (RFQ) via the search engine or your "My List" to ensure current pricing and stock accuracy.

question: Q: What are the pricing criteria and delivery terms for quotations?
answer:
A:
Quotations issued on "synapse" are, as a general rule, calculated based on "EXW" (Ex Works) terms.
Trade Terms & Price Composition

    Standard Incoterms (EXW): Prices reflect the cost of the products at the point of pickup from our warehouse (e.g., Osaka, Japan). This baseline price does not include international freight, customs duties, taxes, or insurance.

    Shipping & Logistics Options:

        Custom Shipping Quotes: Based on your registered shipping address, we can calculate and include international shipping costs (Air, Sea, etc.) as a separate line item in your quotation.

        Buyer-Arranged Pickup: We also accommodate pickups arranged by your designated freight forwarder.

    Quality Maintenance Costs: The cost of rigorous storage and handling under GDP (Good Distribution Practice) standards within our warehouse is already included in the quoted product price.

question: Q: What are the available payment methods and terms?
answer:
A:
To ensure the security of international transactions, payments on "synapse" are, as a general rule, restricted to Advanced Wire Transfer (T/T Advance).
Payment & Financial Protocols

    Accepted Payment Method: Bank Wire Transfer (T/T: Telegraphic Transfer).

        We accept international remittances in major currencies, including USD, EUR, and JPY.

    Payment Timing: 100% Pre-payment (Advance Payment).

        Payment must be completed prior to the dispatch of products, based on the official Invoice issued after order confirmation.

    Banking Fees: All bank charges related to the remittance—including those from the originating, intermediary, and receiving banks (OUR instruction)—are to be borne by the buyer.

    Transaction Security: By adopting an advanced payment system, we eliminate credit risks within the international pharmaceutical supply chain, enabling us to maintain stable supply and competitive pricing.

question: Q: Can you handle products requiring strict temperature control (Cold Chain), such as 2–8°C storage?
answer:
A:
Yes. We provide secure international transportation solutions using state-of-the-art Cold Chain technology to maintain a consistent 2–8°C environment throughout the journey.
Cold Chain Solutions & Quality Assurance

    High-Performance Specialized Containers: We utilize advanced vacuum-insulated containers (e.g., va-Q-tec) and thermal packaging designed to maintain stable temperatures for extended periods during international transit.

    Real-time Temperature Monitoring: Temperature Data Loggers can be included in your shipment to record thermal conditions throughout the flight and inland transit. This provides empirical proof of quality and ensures no temperature deviations occurred upon arrival.

    GDP-Compliant Operations: From our warehouse in Japan to your designated global destination, all handling is conducted under strict EU GDP (Good Distribution Practice) standards.

    Customized Logistics Design: We propose the optimal packaging configuration based on the shipping route, estimated transit time, and the climatic conditions of the destination. Please consult with our specialists during the quotation process for specific requirements.

question: Q: What specific medical field does "Oncology" refer to?
answer:
A:
Oncology is the branch of medicine dedicated to the diagnosis, research, and treatment of cancer (malignant tumors). Within the pharmaceutical and distribution industries, it encompasses specialized anti-cancer drugs (antineoplastics) and the advanced technologies required for their delivery.
Domain Expertise & Sourcing Criticality

    Global High Demand: Japanese-approved oncology products, including small molecule drugs and monoclonal antibodies (mAbs), are in high demand by global medical institutions due to their exceptional quality and clinical reliability.

    Rigorous Quality & Logistics Control: Oncology medications are often high-value and sensitive. They demand strict adherence to GDP (Good Distribution Practice), requiring precise temperature control and specialized handling. A secure supply chain is directly linked to patient outcomes.

    Support for Precision Medicine: As personalized medicine—based on genetic mutations and biomarkers—becomes the standard of care, access to accurate, up-to-date data regarding active ingredients and specific indications is essential for oncologists.

Strategic Value of synapse.net in Oncology

Oncology is a domain where professional expertise and sophisticated logistics intersect. The combination of "synapse's" high-precision English research capabilities and Kosei Pharmaceutical’s secure international distribution infrastructure provides powerful support for cancer treatment worldwide.

question: Q: What are "Rare Diseases"?
answer:
A:
Rare Diseases are medical conditions that affect a very small number of individuals globally, often making diagnosis difficult and treatment options limited. With over 7,000 identified rare diseases worldwide, ensuring patient access to appropriate therapy remains one of the most critical challenges on the front lines of modern medicine.
Social Challenges & Orphan Drugs

    The Diagnostic Odyssey: Due to a lack of specialists and clinical cases, patients often face a "diagnostic maze," taking years to receive an accurate diagnosis. The development and distribution of Orphan Drugs (medications for rare diseases) are urgent global priorities.

    Geographical Access Gaps (Drug Lag): Effective treatments approved in one country may remain unapproved or unavailable in others, leading to a loss of life-critical treatment opportunities.

    Expectations for Japanese Pharmaceuticals: High-quality rare disease medications approved under Japan's rigorous regulatory standards represent a vital lifeline for patients and physicians worldwide.

Social Mission of synapse.net

"synapse" is dedicated to breaking down the "information barriers" and "logistical walls" in rare disease treatment.

    Globalization of Information: By publishing data on Japanese-approved orphan drugs in English, we empower global specialists to conduct rapid, accurate research.

    Supporting the Last Mile: We provide overseas medical institutions with a streamlined procurement route via "My List" and the RFQ process. Our platform supports Expanded Access (Compassionate Use), ensuring that life-saving medicines reach the patients who need them most from a humanitarian perspective.

question: Q: What does "Shortage" refer to in the pharmaceutical industry?
answer:
A:
In the pharmaceutical industry, a Shortage refers to a state of supply deficit, stockouts, or severe scarcity of medications. Caused by factors such as manufacturing disruptions, raw material (API) shortages, or geopolitical risks, the inability to deliver essential drugs to clinical settings has become a critical global challenge.
Supply Chain Resilience & Risk Mitigation

    Information Transparency: For international healthcare professionals, understanding the domestic supply status in Japan is vital for procurement planning. "synapse" publishes a "Medical Supply Information List" reflecting the latest market trends to eliminate information asymmetry.

    Proactive Risk Management: By visualizing detailed statuses such as "Limited Shipment" or "Supply Suspension," we help users avoid stockout issues after ordering, ensuring high-certainty procurement.

    Hub for Alternative Sourcing: When a specific product faces a shortage, we provide rapid information on the availability of therapeutic equivalents or generic alternatives. Leveraging our extensive Japanese network, we support "Continuity of Care" for medical facilities worldwide.

Strategic Advantage of synapse.net

In an era of supply instability, data-driven decision-making is indispensable. "synapse" integrates Kosei Pharmaceutical’s robust domestic inventory with real-time market data, serving as the most reliable supply partner in an uncertain global market.

question: Q: What does it mean to have operations compliant with "EU GDP Standards"?
answer:
A:
GDP (Good Distribution Practice) refers to the international standards governing the appropriate distribution of pharmaceuticals. It is a set of rigorous rules designed to ensure that the quality, efficacy, and safety of a product are maintained throughout the entire process—from leaving the manufacturing plant to reaching the patient.
Global Standards & Quality Assurance

    The "EU Standard": The World's Gold Standard: The GDP guidelines established by the European Union (EU) are recognized as the most stringent in the world. Compliance signifies that the following advanced management systems are in place:

        Strict Temperature Control: Real-time monitoring of temperature throughout storage and transit to prevent the degradation of active ingredients.

        Full Traceability: Complete visualization of the distribution channel to prevent the infiltration of counterfeit medicines, guaranteeing 100% product authenticity.

        Maintenance of Physical Integrity: Utilization of Validated Packaging to prevent contamination or damage, operated within a specialized pharmaceutical-only delivery network.

        GDP-Trained Personnel: All operations are executed by professional staff who undergo continuous training and follow strict Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).

Quality Commitment of synapse.net (Kosei Pharmaceutical)

Our mission is to deliver high-quality Japanese pharmaceuticals to the world while maintaining their Pharmaceutical Integrity exactly as it was at the time of manufacture. Based on the foundation of EU GDP, we provide the highest degree of reliability to medical institutions in all regions, including Asia, the Middle East, and Africa.

question: What are the specific strengths of Japanese pharmaceuticals in the international market?
answer:
A:
The competitive advantage of Japanese pharmaceuticals lies in a world-class Research & Development (R&D) capability combined with "Japan Quality"—a reputation built on some of the world's most stringent quality standards. Japan is one of the few "Major Drug-Discovery Nations" capable of developing innovative new drugs from scratch for the global market.
Four Core Competitive Advantages

    Global Innovation Leadership: Japan consistently pioneers breakthroughs in cutting-edge fields such as Oncology, Regenerative Medicine, and Biopharmaceuticals.

    Excellence in Formulation & Manufacturing: Beyond high-purity active ingredients, Japanese products excel in "Formulation Ingenuity." This includes enhancing patient compliance through ease of swallowing and preventing medical errors through sophisticated labeling and design.

    Rigorous Approval by PMDA: The fact that a product has passed the rigorous examination of the PMDA (Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency) serves as a premium global brand, guaranteeing the highest tiers of safety and efficacy.

    Advanced Life-Science Ecosystem: Japan possesses a robust ecosystem that rapidly translates Nobel-prize-winning basic research (e.g., iPS cells, Immune Checkpoint Inhibitors) into practical clinical applications.

Strategic Value of synapse.net

The information provided on "synapse" is more than just a product catalog; it is an access portal to the core values of Trust, Safety, and High Purity that global buyers demand. Especially in critical, life-saving treatments, Japanese pharmaceuticals serve as the most trusted choice, supporting healthcare professionals on the global front lines.

question: Q: What are the characteristics and benefits of Japan's Drug Pricing System (Official Price)?
answer:
A:
Pharmaceutical prices in Japan are governed by the National Health Insurance (NHI) Drug Price System, where the government determines and manages official prices. This ensures a uniquely stable market structure where high-quality pharmaceuticals are provided at transparent, standardized, and fair prices.
Economic Advantages for Global Buyers

    Superior Cost-Effectiveness: Despite clearing rigorous PMDA reviews and high manufacturing standards (GMP/GDP), Japanese pharmaceuticals remain highly cost-effective compared to other developed markets due to national price controls.

    Price Transparency & Predictability: Unlike markets with high price volatility driven by unconstrained competition, the official price system ensures stability. This allows buyers to conduct procurement with high Predictability, facilitating more accurate budgeting and inventory investment.

    Inflation Resilience & Stable Supply: The system suppresses sudden price spikes caused by external economic factors, maintaining the relative price competitiveness of Japanese products in the international market.

    Incentives for Innovation: Recent trends in the system emphasize the value of Innovative New Drugs (e.g., Oncology and Rare Diseases) while providing incentives for Supply Stability, ensuring that high-reliability products are consistently available.

Strategic Value of synapse.net

"synapse" serves as a trusted gateway that broadcasts real-time data on the latest prices and supply statuses from this highly transparent Japanese market. Backed by Japan's unique and stable regulatory framework, we offer global medical institutions both Peace of Mind and Significant Economic Advantages.

question: Q: How does the "Weak Yen" (Yen depreciation) trend benefit international pharmaceutical buyers?
answer:
A:
The current "Weak Yen" environment presents a Strategic Export Opportunity for international buyers, allowing them to procure high-quality Japanese pharmaceuticals under exceptionally favorable economic conditions.

    Increased Purchasing Power: As the Japanese Yen depreciates, procurement costs in foreign currencies (e.g., USD, EUR) decrease relatively. This allows buyers to secure a larger volume of products within the same budget or reallocate funds toward high-value, cutting-edge medications like Oncology treatments.

    Synergy with the NHI Price System: Japan's unique "Official Drug Pricing" prevents the rapid domestic price spikes often seen in other markets due to inflation. The combination of stable domestic prices and a weak Yen creates the highest level of cost-performance in the global market.

    Uncompromised Quality at Lower Costs: Buyers can benefit from reduced costs due solely to currency factors while maintaining the "Japan Quality" guaranteed by PMDA. This enables healthcare providers to either lower prices for local patients or optimize their own profit margins.

    Hedge Against Global Shortages: A stronger foreign currency budget allows for strategic "Bulk Buying" to build safety stock. In an era of global pharmaceutical supply instability, leveraging the weak Yen to increase inventory is a powerful strategy to enhance your local Supply Chain Resilience.

Strategic Value of synapse.net

"synapse" provides real-time market data and supply statuses. By integrating these insights, buyers can maximize the advantages of currency fluctuations and execute agile, strategic procurement through Kosei Pharmaceutical’s reliable network.

question: Q: I get an "auth.failed" error and cannot log in. What should I do?
answer:
A:
The "auth.failed" error indicates that the email address or password provided does not match our records. Please follow this troubleshooting process to regain access to your account.
Troubleshooting Process

    Re-verify Your Credentials:

        Email Address: Ensure there are no extra spaces and that you are using the correct case (uppercase/lowercase).

        Password: Check if your entry meets the following mandatory security requirements:

            Minimum 8 characters.

            Includes at least one uppercase letter.

            Includes at least one lowercase letter.

            Includes at least one numeric digit.

    Reset Your Password:
    If you are certain the information is correct but still cannot log in, please perform a manual reset:

        Click the "Forgot your password?" link on the login screen.

        Enter your registered email address and click "SEND."

        Follow the instructions in the automated email to set a new, secure password.

Still Having Trouble?

If you do not receive the reset email or continue to encounter the error, please try clearing your browser cache or contact our Customer Support directly for technical assistance.

question: Q: How can I change my registered shipping information or add a new shipping address?
answer:
A:
You can manage your shipping information at any time through the Shipping Address Management menu within your "My Page" dashboard.
Account Management & Address Updates

    Adding a New Shipping Address:

        Log in and navigate to the "My Page" menu.

        Click on "Shipping addresses."

        Click the "Add New Address" button and enter the new details.

    Editing or Updating Existing Information:

        From the "Shipping addresses" list, select the address you wish to modify.

        Click the "Edit" button, update the fields, and click "Save."

Important Considerations

question: Q: Is it possible for individuals (Doctors or Patients) to register an account and purchase products?
answer:
A:
Yes. Registration and purchase for personal importation by Doctors or for personal use by Patients are permitted. However, due to the nature of pharmaceutical products, strict legal requirements and credential verifications are mandatory.
Registration Workflow & Required Documentation

    System Registration (For Doctors & Individuals):

        Industry Type: Even when registering as an individual physician or a patient, please select "Doctor" as the industry type to ensure the process aligns with professional medical oversight.

        Valid Licenses (Mandatory Uploads):

            For Doctors: A valid Medical License.

            For Patients: A valid Prescription issued by your attending physician.

    Import Compliance (Country-Specific Regulations):
    The customs and health authorities of the destination country typically require the following documents for clearance:

        Physician's Letter: An official document justifying the medical necessity of the specific treatment.

        Prescription: Must clearly state the drug name, dosage, and usage instructions.

        Patient Acceptance Letter (Self-Usage Declaration): A sworn statement confirming that the medication is for the designated patient's treatment only and not for resale.

IMPORTANT: Local Regulatory Advisory

Regulations regarding the personal importation of pharmaceuticals vary significantly by country. Due to potential restrictions on unapproved drugs or quantity limits, we strongly recommend that you consult with your local health and customs authorities to verify import eligibility before initiating a transaction.

question: Q: I have a valid prescription. Are there any other documents I need to submit?
answer:
A:
Yes. In addition to the prescription, a copy of the attending physician's Medical License is mandatory.
Credential Verification & Authenticity Assurance

In the international distribution of pharmaceuticals, both documents must be verified as a set to ensure maximum product safety and regulatory compliance.

    Required Documents & Their Roles:

        Prescription: Serves as official proof of the "treatment instructions" for a specific patient.

        Medical License: Serves as official proof that the person issuing those instructions is a "legally qualified medical professional."

    Why the Medical License is Required:

        Verification of Authenticity: To guarantee that the prescription is genuine and issued by a licensed authority.

        Compliance with International Regulations: Customs and health authorities in the destination country typically require proof of the physician’s credentials to grant an Import Permit.

        Safety Protocols: This requirement is based on strict safety management protocols designed to prevent the unauthorized supply of prescription-only medicines to non-professionals.

question: Q: I cannot click the "Continue" button on the cart screen. Why is it disabled?
answer:
A:
If the "Continue" button is inactive (grayed out), it usually means the system has detected that certain validation criteria have not been met. Please check the following potential causes and solutions.
Common Causes & Solutions

    Empty Cart or Improper Selection:
    The button will not activate if the cart is empty or if the items were not added correctly. Please verify that the product names and quantities are visible in your cart.

    Expired Quotation:
    If your cart contains items based on a previously issued estimate, the Expiry Date of that quotation may have passed. Expired quotes are automatically invalidated by the system to ensure pricing accuracy. Please initiate a new Request for Quote (RFQ).

    The "3-Month Rule" Re-verification:
    For security and quality assurance, items added to the cart or purchased more than 3 months ago are locked. This is a safety feature to ensure that you are viewing the most current inventory status, expiry dates, and pricing before proceeding.

Recommended Troubleshooting Steps

    Clear the Cart: Remove (Delete) all items currently in your cart and try adding the products again directly from the product pages.

    Request a Fresh Estimate: For expired or outdated items, click the button to request a new "Estimate" to reflect real-time market data and stock availability.

question: Q: I entered a drug name or active ingredient, but no results are found. Why?
answer:
A:
The most common reason for no search results is the use of non-English characters. Please ensure your search terms are entered in English (Roman alphabet).
Search Algorithm & Database Specifications

    Language Protocol:
    To maintain international accuracy and medical integrity, our database is constructed entirely in English. The system does not support searches in Japanese, Chinese, Arabic, or other non-English languages.

    Recommended Search Methods:

        Brand Name: Enter the product name using the English spelling recognized in the Japanese market.

        Active Ingredient (API / INN): Search using the International Nonproprietary Name (INN) or standard chemical nomenclature in English.

Pro-Tips for Better Search Results

    Check Spelling: Ensure there are no typos in the English spelling.

    Partial Matches: Our algorithm supports partial matches. If a full chemical name is too long or complex, try entering a core part of the word (e.g., "Atorva" for Atorvastatin).

Still Can't Find What You're Looking For?

While we update our database constantly, some of the newest medications approved in Japan may take time to appear. If you require a product not currently listed, please contact us via the Inquiry Form or Messages with the specific English name. We will conduct a manual research and procurement study for you.

question: Q: What do the "Categories" represent on the platform?
answer:
A:
The "Categories" on our platform refer to a structured classification system that organizes our extensive pharmaceutical database based on Therapeutic Areas, Pharmacological Actions, and Regulatory Classifications.
Product Classification & Navigation

    Primary Categorization Criteria:

        Therapeutic Area: Classification by specific disease groups, such as Oncology, Rare Diseases, or Infectious Diseases.

        Pharmacological Class: Classification by the drug’s mechanism of action, such as Antivirals, Immunosuppressants, or Antibiotics.

        Product Modality: Differentiation by specialized forms, including Biopharmaceuticals, Small Molecule Drugs, and Orphan Drugs.

    How to Use:
    By clicking the "Categories" link located directly below the search bar, you can view the full classification list. Selecting a specific domain (e.g., Oncology) allows you to efficiently browse all related products available in the Japanese market.

Benefits of Category-Based Search

    Comprehensive Research: Even if you do not have a specific product name, you can conduct a broad survey of available Japanese medications within a given therapeutic field.

    Specialized Filtering: This feature is ideal for medical specialists and procurement officers who need to rapidly assess the latest supply status within their specific field of expertise.

question: Q: What do the "Tags" assigned to each product represent?
answer:
A:
"Tags" are visual attribute labels that indicate specific logistics requirements, legal regulations, and product classifications for each medication. They are essential for ensuring proper handling and for filtering products based on specific operational criteria.
Attribute Metadata & Regulatory Classifications

    Primary Information Conveyed by Tags:

        Logistics & Storage: Indicators such as "Cold Chain" (2–8°C) or "Controlled Room Temperature" (CRT) that signal the need for specialized international transport.

        Regulatory & Safety Categories: Labels for "Narcotics," "Psychotropics," or "Biologics" that require specific export/import licenses and rigorous handling procedures.

        Product Characteristics: Classifications such as "Generic" or "Orphan Drug" to define the product's market status.

    How to Use:
    Click the "Tags" link located below the search bar to view a complete list of active labels. Selecting a specific tag (e.g., "Cold Chain") will instantly filter the database to show only products meeting those exact conditions.

Benefits of Using Tags

    Proactive Logistics Risk Management: Tags allow you to identify temperature-sensitive or hazardous materials before ordering, making it easier to estimate shipping costs and packaging requirements.

    Enhanced Regulatory Compliance: Clearly identify products—such as narcotics or psychotropic substances—that involve complex import permit processes in your destination country, preventing unforeseen legal delays.

question: Q: How can I find products within a specific therapeutic area using Category Search?
answer:
A:
The Category Search function is designed to help you efficiently filter products based on medical domains (e.g., Oncology, Infectious Diseases) and therapeutic applications.
Navigation & Search Logic

    Step-by-Step Procedure:

        Access via Link: Click the "Categories" link located directly below the search bar on the homepage. This will display a comprehensive list of all registered categories.

        Target Selection: From the list, select the specific medical field you wish to research (e.g., Oncology, Rare Disease, etc.).

        Review the Product List: The system will generate a tailored list of all Japanese pharmaceuticals belonging to that selected category.

    Primary Classification Criteria:

        Therapeutic-Based: Organized according to clinical indications and disease groups.

        Characteristic-Based: Grouped by product modality, such as Biopharmaceuticals or Orphan Drugs.

Benefits of Utilizing Category Search

    Comprehensive Domain Coverage: Even if the exact product name is unknown, you can identify all relevant Japanese medications associated with a specific medical discipline.

    Centralized Supply Insights: It allows you to monitor the latest stock availability and supply statuses (including Shortage alerts) for an entire therapeutic field in one consolidated view.

question: Q: How can I search for products using the Active Ingredient name?
answer:
A:
Our platform allows you to search for all relevant products based on their core Active Ingredient (API) using standard English nomenclature.
Component Search Logic & Operations

    Incremental Search (Auto-Suggest Feature):
    As you type the first few letters of an active ingredient into the search bar, the system will provide a real-time list of matching candidates. This feature ensures quick and accurate results, even for complex chemical names with difficult spellings.

    Active Ingredients List:
    By clicking the "Active Ingredients" link located directly below the search bar, you can access a comprehensive, alphabetized directory of all registered active substances in our database.

    Navigation to Product Listings:
    Clicking a specific ingredient from the suggestions or the list will generate a tailored view of all pharmaceuticals containing that substance, including both Brand-name drugs and Generic alternatives.

Pro-Tips for Successful Searches

    Use International Standards (INN): All searches must be conducted in English. We recommend using International Nonproprietary Names (INN) or standard Romanized chemical names.

    Leverage Partial Matching: Entering even a portion of the ingredient name will trigger the suggestion engine, helping you find the correct substance without needing the full exact spelling.

question: Q: How can I search for products from a specific pharmaceutical manufacturer?
answer:

Pro-Tips for Successful Searches

    Use English Trade Names: All searches must be conducted in English. Please use the official English names of Japanese manufacturers (e.g., Takeda, Astellas, Chugai, Eisai, etc.).

    Researching Corporate Strengths: Selecting a specific manufacturer is an excellent way to gain a bird’s-eye view of their specialized therapeutic areas, helping you understand their core R&D strengths and supply capabilities.

question: Q: How can I find products with specific conditions using Tag Search?
answer:
A:
The Tag Search function allows you to directly extract products based on specific attributes such as temperature control requirements, legal regulations, and product characteristics.
Attribute-Based Filtering

    Step-by-Step Procedure:

        View the Tag List: Click the "Tags" link located directly below the search bar on the homepage. This will display a comprehensive list of all active tags in the system.

        Select an Attribute: Choose the specific condition you wish to research from the list (e.g., Cold Chain, Narcotics, Generic, etc.).

        Review the Results: The system will instantly generate a list of all pharmaceuticals assigned that specific tag.

    Key Information Identified via Tags:

        Logistics Category: Temperature management requirements like "Cold Chain" (2–8°C).

        Regulatory Category: Legal handling classifications such as Narcotics, Psychotropics, or Poisonous Drugs.

        Product Category: Specialized groupings such as Biopharmaceuticals, Orphan Drugs, or Brand-name vs. Generic.

Benefits of Utilizing Tag Search

    Early Identification of Procurement Risks: You can pinpoint products requiring special export/import permits (e.g., Narcotics) or those impacting shipping costs (e.g., Cold Chain) at the very beginning of your search.

    Rapid Response to Specific Needs: Tags allow you to instantly meet targeted procurement mandates, such as "List only high-quality Japanese Generic medicines," streamlining your sourcing workflow.

question: Q: Where can I find the "Logout" option to safely end my session?

    Automatic Timeout: For your protection, the system is configured to log you out automatically after a specific period of inactivity.

    Session Termination: Logging out manually ensures that your temporary authentication session stored in the browser is completely destroyed, preventing unauthorized access.

question: Q: I entered the Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) code, but I still cannot log in. What should I do?
answer:
A:
If you have received the authentication email but the process fails, the most common cause is the "Expiration of the Verification Code." Please follow the troubleshooting flow below to resolve the issue.
Troubleshooting Flow for Authentication Issues

    Request a New Session:
    Verification codes are security-sensitive and remain valid for only a few minutes. If too much time has passed since the email was sent, the code will expire. Please return to the login screen, restart the "Log In" process, and enter the newly issued code immediately.

    Pre-Entry Checklist:

        Single-Byte Characters: Ensure you are entering digits in half-width (alphanumeric) format.

        Check for the Latest Email: If you requested a code multiple times in a short period, ensure you are entering the code from the most recent email. Older codes are automatically invalidated once a new one is requested.

    Handling Delays or Missing Emails:
    If the email does not arrive within a few minutes, please check your Spam/Junk folder or ensure that your mail server is configured to permit emails from our domain (e.g., @kosei.com).

Still Having Trouble?

If the steps above do not resolve the issue, your account may be temporarily locked for security reasons, or there may be a system synchronization error. Please contact us via the Inquiry Form with your full name and registered email address, and our technical team will assist you.

question: Q: How long is the Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) code valid?
answer:
A:
The verification code sent via the 2FA email is valid for 30 minutes from the time of issuance (transmission).
Timeout Management & Security

    Expiration (Timeout) Behavior:
    To ensure account security, any code older than 30 minutes will automatically become Invalid. If you receive an error message after entering the code, it is highly likely that the session has timed out.

    How to Resolve:

        Return to the login screen and re-enter your User ID and password to initiate a new session.

        Check your email for the newly issued verification code and enter it promptly.

    Important Note:
    If you have requested multiple codes within a short timeframe, the system will only recognize the most recent code sent to your inbox. All previous codes are instantly invalidated upon the generation of a new one.

question: Q: How do I use the "Search Options" (Advanced Filtering) feature?
answer:
A:
The "Search Options" feature allows you to refine your search results using specific criteria, enabling you to pinpoint the exact medication you need from our extensive database.
Filtering Logic & Operations

    Multi-faceted Filtering (Desktop):
    From the "Search Options" menu displayed on the search results page, you can apply the following filters:

        Manufacturer: Limit results to specific pharmaceutical companies.

        Active Ingredients: Focus only on products containing specific chemical compounds.

        Therapeutic Category: Extract medications within specific medical fields (e.g., Oncology, Rare Diseases).

        Combinatory Search: You can combine these filters for highly specific research (e.g., "Oncology drugs marketed by Company A").

    Mobile Operations:

    Resetting Filters:

Benefits of Advanced Filtering

    Precision Sourcing: Rapidly narrow down options when dealing with broad active ingredients that may have dozens of brand-name and generic variations.

    Market Mapping: Efficiently analyze the product portfolio of a specific manufacturer within a targeted therapeutic domain.

question: Q: What is the procedure for New Member Registration (Sign-up)?

New Registration & Onboarding Workflow

    Registration Complete (Immediate Access): Your account is now active. You are now authorized to start requesting estimates (Estimate Requests) for any product in our database.

Crucial Requirement: License Registration for Purchasing

While you can request estimates immediately, official pharmaceutical orders (Order Placement) require verification of your legal credentials.

    When to Register: You can upload your credentials at any time after the initial sign-up via the "Company Information" section in "My Page."

    Required Documentation: Please upload a digital copy of your Medical License or relevant business permit. Verification is mandatory to ensure compliance with international pharmaceutical laws.

question: Q: What is the procedure for Logging in?

        Benefit: This saves you from re-entering your email every time, allowing for a faster start to your research.

        Security Advisory: For your protection, do not use this feature on shared or public devices (e.g., internet cafes, shared office terminals).

Having Trouble Logging In?

    Forgot Your Password?: Click the "Forgot password?" link on the login screen to initiate the reset process.

    Two-Factor Authentication (2FA): If you have enabled enhanced security, you will be prompted to enter a verification code sent to your email immediately after providing your password.

question: Q: I cannot log in (an error message is displayed). What should I do?
answer:
A:
Login failures are typically caused by mismatched credentials or forgotten account details. Please follow the troubleshooting steps below based on the specific issue or error message you encounter.
Error Diagnosis & Recovery Flow

    If you see "auth.failed":
    This indicates that either the Email or Password entered does not match our records.

        Double-Check Entries: Ensure there are no accidental spaces, verify capitalizations (case-sensitivity), and confirm that you are using half-width (alphanumeric) characters.

    If you have forgotten your password (Password Reset):

        Click the "Forgot your password?" link on the login screen.

        Enter your registered Email address and click "Send."

        You will receive an automated email containing a password reset link.

        Click the URL provided in the email (Note: The link has an expiration time) and set your new password.

    If you cannot remember your registered Email:
    For security and privacy reasons, our system cannot display registered emails. Please contact us via the Inquiry Form with your Full Name and Organization Name, and our administrative team will assist in identifying your account.

Security Tips

    Account Lockout: For your protection, multiple consecutive failed attempts may result in a temporary account lockout. If this occurs, please wait a few minutes before trying again.

    Password Integrity: When resetting your password, we strongly recommend choosing a unique password that is not reused across other websites to ensure maximum security for your pharmaceutical transactions.

question: Q: I haven’t received the Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) email. What should I do?
answer:
A:
If you cannot find the 2FA verification email, it may be due to email server filtering or storage limitations. Please follow these troubleshooting steps in order.
Troubleshooting Email Delivery Issues

    Check Spam and Promotion Folders:
    Automated system emails are sometimes flagged by security filters and redirected to "Spam," "Junk," or "Promotions" folders.

        Search Tip: Look for emails from the sender domain: noreply@synapse.net.

    Verify Domain Whitelisting (Safe Senders List):
    Corporate email servers or ISPs may block automated system emails at the gateway level.

        Recommended Action: Add @synapse.net to your "Safe Senders" list or ask your IT department to permit (whitelist) incoming emails from this domain.

    Check Your Storage Capacity:
    If your cloud storage (e.g., Gmail, Outlook) or your device's memory is full, you will be unable to receive new messages.

        Solution: Delete unnecessary emails or files to free up space, then attempt to log in again.

Still Having Trouble?

If the code still does not arrive after performing the steps above, or if you suspect your registered email address is incorrect, please contact us via the Inquiry Form with the following information:

    Full Name

    Organization Name (Hospital, Clinic, or Company)

    Approximate Time of Login Attempt

question: Q: I don't know which email address I registered with my account.
answer:
A:

Account Identification & Login Recovery Flow

question: Q: During registration, I received the error "This email has already been taken" and cannot complete my sign-up. What should I do?
answer:
A:
This message indicates that the Email Address you entered is already registered as an active account in our system. Please follow the resolution flow based on your situation.
Resolution Flow for Duplication Errors

    If You May Have Registered in the Past:
    Our system does not allow multiple accounts to be created with the same email address. If you have previously completed a trial or full registration, you must use that existing account.

        Solution: Go to the login screen and click the "Forgot your password?" link. If the address is registered, you will receive a password reset email, allowing you to regain access immediately.

    If You Don't Recall Registering (or the Address is Shared Within Your Organization):

Quick Search Tips

question: Q: Why are some fields in my registered information not editable?

    To Change Your Permissions (Role):

question: Q: Are there any membership registration fees or monthly/annual dues for using the site?

question: Q: I am not receiving the registration confirmation or system notification emails. What should I do?

question: Q: How can I search for products (pharmaceuticals)?
answer:
A:
On "synapse," we utilize a Dual-Search Logic that allows you to either find a specific product via "Keyword Search" or explore broad options via "Category & List Browsing."
1. Direct Keyword Search

Enter any of the following details into the search bar on the homepage and click the "Magnifying Glass Icon" (or press Enter):

    Brand Name: The commercial name of the medication (using the Roman alphabet).

2. Browsing via Conditions & Lists

    Categories: Best for finding drugs by therapeutic area (e.g., Oncology, Rare Diseases).

    Manufacturers: Best for viewing the complete product lineup of a specific pharmaceutical company.

    Tags: Best for identifying products with specific attributes, such as "Cold Chain" requirements or "Narcotic" classifications.

    Active Ingredients: Best for comparing different brands or generic alternatives that contain the same core substance.

Pro-Tips for Successful Searching

    Input in English: Our database is entirely English-based. Please ensure all search queries are entered using the Roman alphabet.

    Refinement with Search Options: After your initial search, use the "Search Options" menu to apply granular filters and narrow down your results even further.

question: Q: Is it possible to obtain a Certificate of Origin (COO)?
answer:
A:
Yes, we can issue a Certificate of Origin. Please request it during the ordering process by following the steps below.
Ordering Process & Document Requests

    How to Request:
    After adding products to your cart, locate the "Certificate of Origin" checkbox on the Cart Page and ensure it is selected.

    Issuance Timing:
    Once your Order is finalized with the checkbox selected, we will begin preparing the Certificate of Origin in parallel with the shipping process.

Important Considerations

    Lead Time: Since issuance may require procedures with external organizations such as the Chamber of Commerce, several additional business days may be added to the standard shipping schedule.

    Costs: Separate issuance fees or Legalization (Notary) fees may apply. Please verify the specific conditions within your cart or the terms provided in your quotation.

Pro-Tips for Trade Documentation

    Specific Formats: If your destination country requires a specific form or a Consular Invoice, please consult with us in advance via the Messages feature or the Inquiry Form.

    Hard Copy vs. Soft Copy: We recommend confirming whether you require the physical original (Hard copy) by mail or if a digital version (Soft copy/PDF) is sufficient for your local customs clearance.

question: Q: Can you translate the Package Insert (Prescribing Information) into English or other languages?
answer:
A:
We apologize, but we do not provide translations or multilingual versions of the Package Insert.
Logic for Safety and Compliance

    Importance of Safety Information:
    The Package Insert contains critical data directly linked to patient safety and life, such as Side Effects, Contraindications, and Dosage and Administration.

    Mitigating Translation Risks:
    In medical communication, even a minor nuance error in translation can lead to severe medical accidents. Prioritizing safety above all, we handle only the manufacturer’s original text as the official document to prevent any alteration of information caused by secondary translations.

How to Access Information

Please refer directly to the original version in the official language provided with the product, or consult the official documents and resources published by the manufacturer on their website.

question: Q: Is it possible to receive photos of the product packaging (outer box) before purchasing?
answer:
A:
Yes, we will respond as flexibly as possible. However, the availability of photos depends on the product's inventory status and supplier constraints.
Physical Verification: Flexibility and Constraints

    For products currently in our stock or those we handle on a regular basis, we can provide actual photos of the product packaging.

    Restricted or Delayed Cases:
    For medications we are handling for the first time or "On-request items" (ordered from suppliers only after receiving your request), we cannot confirm the physical product until it arrives at our facility. Therefore, pre-shipment photos may not be immediately available.

    Content Provided:
    Generally, we provide photos of the entire outer package. If you require verification of a specific Batch Number (Lot No.) or Expiry Date, this may only be possible during the inspection phase after the order is finalized.

How to Request Photos

If you require photos for customs pre-clearance or to verify specific product specifications, please send a request via Messages or the Inquiry Form. Please include:

    The Product Name

    The Specific Area to be Photographed (e.g., active ingredient list, barcode section, etc.)

question: Q: Can I request a temperature logger to monitor the products during transit?
answer:
A:
Yes, temperature loggers are available as a paid option. This ensures full traceability and guarantees product quality during the transportation of temperature-sensitive pharmaceuticals.
Temperature Management Logic & Cost Structure

    Service Overview:
    We place a temperature data logger inside the shipping container to record temperature fluctuations from the moment of dispatch to delivery. Upon arrival, the data can be retrieved to verify that the required temperature range (e.g., 2°C–8°C) was strictly maintained.

    Fee Structure:
    The use of a logger is an optional service. Costs vary depending on whether it is a rental unit or a single-use disposable device.

        How to Request: Select the logger option on the Cart Page (or the final confirmation screen) before finalizing your order. Alternatively, you can request it directly from your representative via Messages.

    Types of Loggers:
    We select the most appropriate device (e.g., USB-readable or Cloud-linked) based on the destination and the expected duration of transit.

Benefits of Using a Data Logger

    Quality Assurance: Provides objective, data-driven proof of product stability upon arrival.

    Risk Management: In the event of transit delays or external issues, the logger allows for an accurate assessment of any potential impact on the medication's efficacy.

question: Q: Is it possible to request shipping using high-performance insulated containers (such as va-Q-tec)?
answer:
A:
Yes, this is available as a paid option. It is a highly reliable transport solution for international shipments requiring extended temperature stability or for products with exceptionally strict temperature requirements.
Advanced Logistics Solution: Operational Models

    Service Models:

        Handled by Us: As a paid option, we can arrange and prepare the high-performance container on your behalf.

        Customer-Provided Materials: In certain cases, you may provide your own containers (e.g., va-Q-tec). You can send the packaging materials to our facility in advance, and we will use them to pack and ship your order.

    Key Benefits:
    Equipped with vacuum insulation panels, these containers can maintain specific temperature ranges (e.g., 2°C to 8°C or -15°C to -25°C) for several days—up to nearly 200 hours—without an external power source.

    Costs and Procedures:
    Usage fees vary based on container size and transit distance. Additionally, since these are often rental units, a return logistics flow must be established. Please consult us via Messages or the Inquiry Form for specific details.

Decision-Making Advice

    Recommended Use Cases: Ideal for regions with long transit times or for high-value products where the risk of temperature excursion in standard insulated boxes is too high.

    Advance Coordination: If you are providing your own packaging materials, careful coordination is required between the arrival of your materials at our warehouse and the scheduled product dispatch. Please allow ample time for consultation.

question: Q: What are PIL (Package Information Leaflet) and SmPC (Summary of Product Characteristics)?
answer:
A:
These refer to the Package Insert (comprehensive information materials including labeling and prescribing information). Our platform provides these official documents in their original Japanese format, covering the following essential data:

    Mechanism of Action, Indications, and Usage

    Dosage and Administration

    Contraindications, Warnings, and Precautions

    Adverse Reactions and Safety Information

    Storage Conditions and Expiration Dates

Our Policy on Translation and Safety

Regarding English translations: In medical communication, even a minor nuance error in translation can lead to severe medical accidents.

To prioritize patient safety and prevent any alteration of information caused by secondary translations, we handle only the manufacturer’s original Japanese text as the sole official and authentic document. We do not provide in-house translations to ensure the absolute integrity of the medical data provided by the pharmaceutical companies.

question: Q: Can you issue the "Invoice" required for customs clearance and payment?

question: Q: How do I attach and send files using the Message feature?
answer:
A:
You can send files via the "Messages" page by following the steps below. This feature is useful for replying to quotes or sharing product specifications.
Messaging Operations & Attachment Workflow

    Select a Message Room:
    Go to the Messages page and select the relevant room for your inquiry.

        Filter Function: You can filter conversations by All, Direct Message, Quote Request, or Orders to find the correct thread quickly.

    Compose Your Message:
    Click on the subject line in the list to open the message composition screen (thread).
    3.  Attach and Send:

Restrictions on Attachments

question: Q: Are there any limits on the file formats or sizes I can send via messages?
answer:
A:
Yes. To maintain system stability and high security standards, the following restrictions are applied to all file attachments within our messaging system.
Technical Specifications for File Attachments

    Maximum File Size:
    Up to 5MB per file.

    Supported File Formats:

        Images: JPG, PNG

        Documents: PDF, Word, Excel

    Unsupported Formats:
    For security reasons, compressed files ( .zip) and executable files ( .exe) cannot be attached. If you need to share information contained in these formats, please convert the content to a PDF before sending.

Solutions for Exceeding the Size Limit

If you need to send high-resolution photos or documents that exceed the 5MB limit, please try the following methods:

    Optimize PDFs: Use the "Compress PDF" or "Reduce File Size" feature in your document software.

    Resize Images: Use online tools or apps to reduce the resolution of images before attaching them.

    Send in Multiple Parts: Split your data into smaller files and send them across several messages.

question: Q: I get an error when trying to attach a file to a message. Why can't I send it?
answer:
A:
If your file attachment fails to send, it is likely due to the file format, size, or an issue with the file extension. Please check the following items to resolve the issue.
Troubleshooting and Resolution Steps

    Verify File Format and Size:
    Ensure your file meets our system specifications:

        Supported Formats: PDF, Word, Excel, PNG, JPG

        Maximum Size: Up to 5MB per file

    Rename File Extensions (For .jpeg Files):
    In some environments, files with the ".jpeg" extension may not be correctly recognized.

        Solution: Change the end of the filename (the extension) from ".jpeg" to ".jpg" and try uploading the file again.

    Additional Checkpoints:

        Filename Characters: Files containing special symbols or non-standard characters may cause errors. Rename the file using only simple alphanumeric characters.

        Connection Stability: Large files (close to 5MB) may time out if your network connection is unstable.

Still Having Trouble?

If you still cannot send the file after trying the steps above, there may be a temporary system glitch or a compatibility issue with your specific environment. Please contact us via the Inquiry Form with the following details:

    The exact error message displayed

    The format and size of the file you attempted to send

    The browser you are using (e.g., Chrome, Safari)

question: Q: How do I open an attachment (file) received in a message?

question: Q: What exactly does "Licenses" refer to on this platform?
answer:
A:
In the context of "synapse," Licenses refer to the official business permits issued by national regulatory authorities (such as the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare in Japan or the FDA/EMA in other regions). These are mandatory for the proper and safe handling of pharmaceuticals and medical devices.
Systematic Understanding of Pharmaceutical Licenses

The distribution of pharmaceuticals is strictly regulated by law, requiring appropriate licenses based on the specific role of the business (manufacturing, importing, or selling). Key examples include:

    Wholesale License: Required for wholesaling pharmaceuticals to medical institutions or other authorized distributors.

    Manufacturing License: Required for the production or final packaging/labeling of medicinal products.

    Import License: Required to legally import pharmaceuticals from overseas and distribute them within a specific domestic market.

    Pharmacy / Medical Institution License: Required for pharmacies or hospitals to dispense and prescribe medications directly to patients.

Why is the submission of these licenses mandatory?

Because pharmaceuticals directly impact human life, global trade is legally permitted only between "Authorized Entities" who possess valid qualifications.

By verifying that every user on "synapse" holds the appropriate licenses, we ensure strict compliance with international laws. This process is vital for:

    Preventing the infiltration of counterfeit medicines.

    Building a secure and transparent supply chain.

    Guaranteeing the integrity of the products being traded.

question: Q: What specific documents are required for the "Licenses" submission?
answer:
A:
The required documents for pharmaceutical trading are strictly categorized based on your "Country" and "Business Type" (e.g., Wholesaler, Hospital, Pharmacy). Please follow the steps below to identify the exact documents required for your organization.
How to Identify Your Required Documents

    Check the Official Document List:
    Go to the Licenses submission page and click the following link:

        Verification Link: "View: Table 1 - List of documents to be requested at the time of inquiry"

        This link contains the most up-to-date matrix of required documents based on the legal regulations of each country.

    Examples of Common Documents (By Business Type):
    While specific requirements vary, representative documents typically include:

        Wholesaler: Wholesale Distribution Authorization (WDA) or equivalent.

        Hospital / Pharmacy: Facility Establishment Permit or a license for dispensing/prescribing medications.

        Manufacturer: Manufacturing License or GMP Certificate.

    Checkpoints Before Uploading:

        Language: Documents should ideally be in English or the official language of the issuing country.

        Validity: The documents must be current and within their expiration date.

        Consistency: Ensure the Organization Name and Address on the document exactly match the information registered on our site.

Still Unsure Which Document to Use?

If you have reviewed "Table 1" but remain unsure which specific document from your country applies, please contact us via Messages or the Inquiry Form. Be sure to specify your Country and Business Type.

question: Q: When will my uploaded licenses be approved?
answer:
A:
Your submitted license documents are reviewed sequentially by our licensed pharmacists and compliance officers. Approval typically takes 2 to 3 business days after the upload is complete.
Review Process and Notification Flow

    Stages of the Review:

        Submitted: The state immediately after you have uploaded your documents.

        Under Review: Our specialized staff is verifying the document's validity, expiration date, and consistency with your registered information.

        Approved: Once approved, the license information is officially reflected in your account.

    If Discrepancies are Found:
    If documents are illegible, insufficient, or expired, the status will be marked as "Rejected."

        Notification: You will receive an email at your registered address detailing the issue and providing instructions for resubmission.

        Next Steps: Please review the email and upload the corrected documents accordingly.

What if I don't hear back after 3 business days?

If you have not received a notification, there may be a network issue or the email might have been filtered into your spam folder.

    Internal Check: Go to the Messages page on the platform to see if there is any direct correspondence from our support team.

    Direct Inquiry: If you still cannot confirm your status, please contact us via the Inquiry Form with your Company Name.

question: Q: I have uploaded my licenses. How can I confirm they were received correctly?
answer:
A:
You can monitor the real-time status of your uploaded documents directly on the Licenses management page.
How to Check Your Application Status

    Access the License Management Page:

        On PC: Click the user icon at the far right of the header and select "Licenses."

        On Smartphone: Tap the menu button (three-bar icon) at the top left and select "Licenses."

    Understand Your Status:

        Approved: Verification is complete, and the license is officially accepted.

        Rejected: The document was not accepted due to discrepancies. You will receive a separate email with specific details.

If Your Documents Are Not Displayed

If you do not see the documents you believe you uploaded, the transmission may have failed due to a connection error or timeout during the upload process.

    Solution: Please attempt to upload the files again using the "Submit licenses" button.

    Check: Ensure your file size is under 5MB and the format is PDF, JPG, or PNG.

question: Q: Is it possible to obtain a CoA (Certificate of Analysis) for a product?
answer:
A:
We apologize, but we do not provide a Certificate of Analysis (CoA). Our policy is based on the following pharmaceutical quality assurance standards.
Quality Assurance & Information Integrity

    Strict Manufacturer Control:
    A CoA is a quality certificate issued by the pharmaceutical manufacturer for each specific production batch. These are highly confidential documents usually disclosed only to direct primary distributors.

    Integrity of the Distribution Process:
    Our primary mission is to deliver products while maintaining the original quality guaranteed by the manufacturer. Since we do not perform independent laboratory testing, we do not issue analysis certificates under our own name.

How Quality is Guaranteed

The quality and authenticity of the product are managed and guaranteed by the Expiry Date and Batch/Lot Number printed on the original manufacturer's packaging. If you require detailed chemical analysis data, we recommend contacting the manufacturer directly using these identifiers.

question: Q: What information should be included in a "Physician Letter" required for the personal import of pharmaceuticals?
answer:
A:
While customs clearance is determined on a case-by-case basis, insufficient detail in a Physician Letter significantly increases the risk of shipment seizure or delays. In practice, documentation that reflects "personal import by the patient under the physician's supervision" tends to clear customs more smoothly than "import by the physician for stock."
Critical Items to Increase Customs Clearance Success

Based on international personal import policies (such as the US FDA's Personal Importation Policy), the following details are practically essential:

    Patient Information: Full name, address, date of birth, and clinical diagnosis.

    Medical Necessity: A clear explanation of why domestically available alternatives are insufficient or inappropriate for this specific patient.

    Statement of Supervision: An explicit statement that the imported medication will be used under the direct supervision of the signing physician for the treatment of the specified patient.

    Product Details: Accurate brand name, active ingredients, dosage, administration instructions, and the quantity to be imported (typically limited to a 90-day supply).

    Safety Commitment: A statement confirming that the physician has verified the safety of the medication and will take responsibility for monitoring and responding to any adverse reactions.

    Signature & Contact Details: The physician's original signature, professional letterhead of the medical institution, and a direct contact phone number.

Practical Advice for Success

    The "Patient-Led, Doctor-Supervised" Framework: Most customs authorities prefer the logic that the patient is importing the medicine for their own life-saving treatment, with the physician acting as the professional overseer. Framing the request as "importing for clinic stock" often triggers stricter commercial regulations.

    Accessing Templates: If you require a specific template or sample draft, please contact us via Messages and request a "Physician Letter Sample." We can provide a structured draft based on successful practical examples.

question: Q: Who is responsible for creating the "Invoice" required for import and export?
answer:
A:
We (the shipper) handle the creation and arrangement of all necessary invoices. You do not need to prepare these documents yourself.
Responsibility and Expertise in Documentation

    Our Scope of Support:
    We prepare the Commercial Invoice (CI) and Packing List (PL) essential for international transport, ensuring they are formatted to comply with global customs regulations.

    Accuracy of Information:
    To facilitate smooth customs clearance for pharmaceuticals, we ensure that all details—including the official product name, quantity, unit price, Incoterms (trade terms), and country of origin—are accurately recorded.

    Digital Accessibility:
    Once the shipment is complete, you can view and download the generated invoice in PDF format from your Order History on My Page.

Pro-Tips for Trade Operations

    Proforma Invoice (PI): If you require a "provisional invoice" for payment procedures (such as obtaining a wire transfer permit) before finalizing your order, please request one via Messages.

    Special Requirements: If your country's regulations require specific declarations, additional wording, or Notarization/Legalization, please consult us in advance. We are happy to accommodate these requests to ensure compliance with your local laws.

question: Q: Who handles the creation and arrangement of the Air Waybill (AWB)?
answer:
A:
We (the shipper) handle the creation and arrangement of the AWB. We manage the contracts with carriers (such as DHL, FedEx, etc.) and issue all necessary shipping labels, so no procedures are required on your part.
Logistics Management & Tracking

    Our Scope of Support:
    As soon as your order is ready for dispatch, we issue the Air Waybill (AWB). This document contains essential information including shipper/consignee details, delivery address, piece count, weight, and the unique Tracking Number.

    Monitoring Shipment Progress:
    The tracking number is finalized the moment the AWB is issued. Once the shipment is dispatched, you can track its progress in real-time via the automated notification email sent by our system or through your Order History on My Page.

    Role in Customs Clearance:
    The AWB serves as a critical piece of evidence alongside the Commercial Invoice, acting as the primary contract of carriage during customs inspections in the destination country.

Pro-Tips for Trade Operations

    Specifying Direct or Forwarded Delivery: The AWB lists the Consignee (receiver) information exactly as provided. If you require direct delivery to a hospital/clinic or a specific freight forwarder, please ensure the delivery address is correctly designated before finalizing your order.

    Accessing Digital Copies: If you require a copy of the AWB for your records, you can view the detailed status on the carrier's website via the link in your dispatch notification. For a specific PDF copy, please request one through Messages.

question: Q: How is the FDA declaration (Prior Notice for pharmaceuticals, etc.) handled for shipments to the U.S.?
answer:
A:
For shipments to the United States, the FDA declaration process is handled by the carrier (DHL, FedEx, UPS, etc.) acting as your customs broker. You do not need to file a declaration directly with the FDA yourself.
Responsibility and Workflow for U.S. Customs Clearance

    The Carrier's Role:
    Using their internal customs brokerage department, the carrier handles the Prior Notice (PN) filing and the submission of required information to the FDA based on the commercial invoice and documents prepared by us.

    The Consignee's (Your) Role:
    During the clearance process, the FDA may request additional details, such as the "Purpose of Import" or a "Physician's Prescription." In such cases, please provide the requested information promptly following the instructions from the carrier.

    Our Role:
    To ensure a smooth FDA declaration, we prepare and attach accurate customs documentation that clearly specifies the active ingredients and the intended use of the products.

Practical Considerations

    Prior Notice (PN): While a PN number is mandatory for importing pharmaceuticals or supplements into the U.S., this is typically processed automatically within the carrier’s logistics system.

    Risk of Import Refusal: If the FDA determines that a shipment exceeds personal importation limits (in terms of quantity or specific ingredients), there is a risk that the package may be returned or destroyed.
